title: Apart from competency, legal translation demands in-depth knowledge of legal and linguistic systems particularly when culture-specific and system-based terms are embedded.

evidence.raw_text:
Mohamed Abdou Moindjie & Ilangko Subramaniam  
 
ABSTRACT  
 
This study is about the translatability of Arabic religious legal terms in Palestinian divorce 
documents. It is a qualitative descriptive study which aims at exploring the translator’s choices 
and decisions in dealing with divorce terminologies to dete
evidence.clean_text: Apart from competency, legal translation demands in -depth knowledge of legal and linguistic systems particularly when culture -specific and system -based terms are embedded. Notably, one should be knowledgeable in the syntax and semantic rules of both the source and the target texts (El-Farahaty, 2016; Ali, 2016).
